items.title: Archive of Byte magazine, starting with issue #1 in 1975
items.author: DamnInteresting
items.summary: Byte magazine began in 1975, focusing on personal computing, and ceased publication in 1998. Its first issue featured articles on early personal computers. Archive.org hosts its early issues for free access.

items.title: Notion leaks email addresses of all editors of any public page
items.author: Tiberium
items.summary: Public Notion pages leak email addresses of all editors without any authentication. This vulnerability allows anyone to access editor details. This issue persists despite no reported fixes.

items.title: The seven programming ur-languages (2022)
items.author: helloplanets
items.summary: The seven programming ur-languages in 2022 are Lisp, ML, Forth, Scheme, Algol, APL, and Emacs Lisp. These languages are foundational in their unique approaches to programming and language design.

items.title: Game devs explain the tricks involved with letting you pause a game
items.author: speckx
items.summary: Game developers typically pause games by setting the game's timescale to zero and ensuring menu UI functions work. Some games use multiple pause levels and freeze time for screenshots behind the pause menu. Pausing is technically complex and varies by game.

items.title: Pairwise Order of a Sequence of Elements
items.author: ibobev
items.summary: The pairwise order of a sequence determines the relative order of elements based on given comparisons. A unique ordering requires a complete set of comparisons. Algorithms can generate optimal orders based on pairwise scores.

items.title: Turtle WoW classic server announces shutdown after Blizzard wins injunction
items.author: Brajeshwar
items.summary: Turtle WoW servers will close on May 14, 2026, after Blizzard's successful lawsuit. All servers have been updated to the final patch. Social media and forums will shut down on October 16, 2026.

items.title: College instructor turns to typewriters to curb AI-written work
items.author: gnabgib
items.summary: A college instructor has started using typewriters in class to reduce AI-generated student work. This method aims to enhance traditional writing skills. The approach is gaining attention for its effectiveness.

items.title: Minimal Viable Programs (2014)
items.author: bachmeier
items.summary: A minimal viable program is the smallest, simplest version of a program that solves a specific problem. Joe Armstrong's 2014 article on minimal viable programs emphasizes its importance in software development. The concept remains relevant for efficient problem-solving.

items.title: Show HN: Prompt-to-Excalidraw demo with Gemma 4 E2B in the browser (3.1GB)
items.author: teamchong
items.summary: A new demo shows how to turn prompts into Excalidraw drawings using Gemma 4 E2B in the browser, requiring 3.1GB. It's available at teamchong.github.io/turboquant-wasm/draw.html. This demo is gaining attention on Hacker News and Reddit.

items.title: The electromechanical angle computer inside the B-52 bomber's star tracker
items.author: NelsonMinar
items.summary: The electromechanical angle computer in the B-52 bomber's star tracker calculates the aircraft's position using celestial navigation. It determines the azimuth and altitude of stars for precise navigation. This system uses angles to locate the aircraft relative to the stars.

items.title: Airline worker arrested after sharing photos of bomb damage in WhatsApp group
items.author: aa_is_op
items.summary: An airline worker in Dubai was arrested for sharing bomb damage photos on WhatsApp. Authorities monitored the private group and lured him to a meeting for arrest. This incident highlights surveillance practices in Dubai.

items.title: Why Japan has such good railways
items.author: RickJWagner
items.summary: Japan's excellent railway system results from effective public policy, including sound regulation and privatization models, fostering high trust and integrity among citizens and employees. Despite some rural lines closing, the overall system remains highly efficient and reliable.

items.title: Matt Mullenweg Overrules Core Committers; Puts Akismet on WP 7's Connector List
items.author: mooreds
items.summary: Matt Mullenweg overruled core committers to include Akismet on WordPress 7.0's Connectors Screen. This decision was controversial and widely discussed. The inclusion was finalized despite opposition.

items.title: The world in which IPv6 was a good design (2017)
items.author: signa11
items.summary: In 2017, IPv6 was seen as good design in a world without address shortages and without the need for translation layers. It aimed to eliminate MAC addresses and ARP/DHCP, reducing network overhead.

items.title: Modern Common Lisp with FSet
items.author: larve
items.summary: Modern Common Lisp with FSet enhances the language with functional programming tools, complementing its traditional object-oriented and imperative paradigms. FSet is notable for introducing functional algorithms to Common Lisp. The book aims to educate programmers in functional thinking.

items.title: Optimizing Ruby Path Methods
items.author: weaksauce
items.summary: Ruby path methods can be optimized by using Bootsnap to reduce load path searches, and since Ruby 4.1.0, `File.join` is faster than string interpolation for simple paths. These optimizations significantly improve performance.

items.title: Spiral staircase with a single guardrail once led to the top of the Eiffel Tower
items.author: bookofjoe
items.summary: A spiral staircase with a single guardrail once led to the top of the Eiffel Tower. It was dismantled in 1983 and now some steps are owned by a private collector. The Eiffel Tower now uses elevators for access.

items.title: Bypassing the kernel for 56ns cross-language IPC
items.author: riyaneel
items.summary: Futexes are used optionally for inter-process communication, not bypassing the kernel entirely. The method achieves 56ns latency. Futexes allow processes to sleep without full kernel involvement.

items.title: The Trouble with Transformers
items.author: rob_lh
items.summary: Transformer failures often result from overloads and short circuits on the secondary side. Transformers are increasingly hard to source, affecting infrastructure projects. This scarcity impacts electric vehicle infrastructure and other utilities.

items.title: Thoughts and feelings around Claude Design
items.author: cdrnsf
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new design tool rivaling Figma and Canva, integrating AI for coding and design systems. Users describe it as innovative, with features like chat refinement and custom sliders. It's seen as a significant step in design tooling.

items.title: Scientists discover “cleaner ants” that groom giant ants in Arizona desert
items.author: t-3
items.summary: Cleaner ants in the Arizona desert groom larger harvester ants without being attacked. This mutualistic behavior is akin to cleaner fish in the ocean. Scientists documented over 90 interactions of this new ant cleaning behavior.
